person.name: Mrs. William Stebbing
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
=== By Roland Henry Baker III | 20 February 2022 ===

Coddington, John Insley. Stebbing Family of Co. Essex, England, and Rowland, Martin, Edward and Editha Stebbing or Stebbins of New England. American Genealogist (D.L. Jacobus). (Oct 1955) 31:199

William Stebbing of Black Notley, Essex has no baptism records or burial records. We do not know his parents or the name of his wife. He had five children baptized there between 1 Nov 1593 and 18 Mar 1603/4 when he left the parish. He may be the man of the same name in the Braintree Vestry Book 6 Sep 1619. His known children in Black Notley are Ellen, Edward, Amy, Thomas and Margaret. If he was identical to the man of Braintree then also Edith in 1610. 

Everything else you read about him including his parents, his wife's name and his birth and death dates are completely fabricated and copied over the Internet by non-genealogists. Records from Suffolk do not belong to this person.

=== Links to Peer-Reviewed Scholarly Sources  ===

The American Genealogist. New Haven, CT: D. L. Jacobus, 1932-. (Online database. AmericanAncestors.org. New England Historic Genealogical Society, 2009 - .)

https://www.americanancestors.org/DB283/i/11830/199/0

The Great Migration Begins: Immigrants to New England 1620-1633, Volumes I-III. (Online database: AmericanAncestors.org, New England Historic Genealogical Society, 2010), (Originally Published as: New England Historic Genealogical Society. Robert Charles Anderson, The Great Migration Begins: Immigrants to New England 1620-1633, Volumes I-III, 3 vols., 1995).

https://www.americanancestors.org/DB393/i/12107/1753/1415520631

zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Stebbing於大約1571年出生在英國。她於大約1592年與William Stebbing在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Stebbing
meanings: Anglo-Saxon: Steb, a boll, a trunk, and ing, originating from, meaning offspring of a trunk, a shoot, a branch

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Stebbing was born about 1571, in England. She married William Stebbing about 1592, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Stebbing est née environ 1571, à Angleterre. Elle a épousé William Stebbing environ 1592, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Stebbing wurde um 1571 in England geboren. Sie hat um 1592 in Essex, England William Stebbing geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 5 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Stebbing è nata il circa 1571 nel England. Lei sposato William Stebbing circa 1592, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 5 figlie.

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Stebbing(은)는 대략 1571년 영국에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1592년 영국 에세스에서 William Stebbing(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Stebbing nasceu aproximadamente 1571, em Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com William Stebbing aproximadamente 1592, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 5 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Stebbing родилась около 1571 г. в местности Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Stebbing около 1592 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Stebbing nació aproximadamente 1571, en Inglaterra. Se casó con William Stebbing aproximadamente 1592, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 5 hijas.
